name: port-allocator
description: Automatically allocate and manage development server ports, avoiding port conflicts between multiple Claude Code instances
capabilities: Allocates unique 10-port ranges to projects with package.json
Scans code directories to discover and register new projects
Lists all allocated ports across registered projects
Auto-detects common code directory locations on first run
Configures Claude Code permissions for port management commands
author: guo-yu
securitySummary: All 298 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only markdown documentation files that instruct Claude on port management. The backticks are code examples in markdown, not executable code. The 'weak crypto' detections are matching 'MD' in filenames. High entropy in Japanese/Chinese files is expected for non-ASCII text.
valueStatement: Running multiple development projects leads to port conflicts and confusion. Port Allocator assigns unique port ranges to each project automatically.
userTitle: Manage Development Server Ports Across Projects
limitations: Only works with projects containing package.json files
Port ranges start at 3000 and increment by 10
Requires manual configuration if code directory is non-standard
Does not monitor or restart services automatically

useCases.title: Manage Multiple Project Ports
useCases.description: Run several web applications simultaneously without port conflicts by assigning each project its own dedicated port range.

useCases.title: Standardize Port Assignments
useCases.description: Create consistent port allocation across team members so everyone uses the same ports for shared projects.

useCases.title: Organize Microservices Ports
useCases.description: Allocate port ranges for services in a microservices architecture with main app, API, and auxiliary service ports.

promptTemplates.title: Get Current Project Port
promptTemplates.scenario: Find the allocated port for your current project

promptTemplates.title: List All Ports
promptTemplates.scenario: See all registered projects and their port ranges

promptTemplates.title: Scan for New Projects
promptTemplates.scenario: Discover and register new projects in your code directory

promptTemplates.title: Configure Code Directory
promptTemplates.scenario: Set a custom path for your main code directory

bestPractices: Run /port-allocator allow first to configure permissions and avoid confirmation prompts
Use /port-allocator scan after adding new projects to your code directory
Only kill processes within your project port range when restarting services
antiPatterns: Do not use pkill -f node as it will affect other projects
Do not manually assign ports outside your allocated range
Do not overwrite ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md - always append to preserve existing content

faq.question: What port range will my project get?
faq.answer: Each project gets 10 consecutive ports starting from 3000. The first project gets 3000-3009, second gets 3010-3019, and so on.

faq.question: What if my project has no package.json?
faq.answer: The skill only allocates ports to directories containing package.json. Add a package.json or use /port-allocator add to manually register.

faq.question: How do I change my code directory?
faq.answer: Run /port-allocator config ~/your/path to set a custom code directory location.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-Node.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need a package.json file or use the manual add command to register the project.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of ports?
faq.answer: With 10-port ranges starting at 3000, you can have approximately 700 projects before reaching port 10000.

faq.question: Does this work across multiple Claude Code instances?
faq.answer: Yes, the port registry in ~/.claude/port-registry.json is shared across all Claude Code instances on your machine.
